content: The intrinsic reward prediction error signals that power curiosity-driven methods like ICM and RND can be repurposed as a dynamic memory pruning gate for state-of-the-art active learning agent memory systems, where only surprising, unpredicted transitions that trigger curiosity are retained in long-term episodic memory to reduce bloat and preserve planning efficiency. This framework turns the question of 'which curious agent architecture produces more meaningful prediction errors' into a direct performance lever for active learning memory efficiency.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
